options.text: Data is kept mostly in employee systems in spreadsheets and logs
Transaction data from a siloed systems is in use in the organization

Organization procures trusted external data to augment and enrich its own datasets

Organization considers technology as a cost function and makes investment based on core - financial, regulatory or specific customer requirements
Organization makes some technology investments outside of core requirements but those are internally focused and made for improving process efficiencies

There is an understanding that technology is a key business enabler and decisions on technology is both external and internal facing. A customer metric based business case is required for investment decision
Company has adopted a forward looking platform approach to technology. The technology backbone supports rapid development and deployment of products and services

functional.text: How is raw material receiving (inwarding) currently documented?

options.text: No records; verbal confirmation only
Manual gate entry register maintained
Excel-based GRN (Goods Receipt Note) created per consignment
ERP-based GRN raised automatically on receipt
Digital gate-in system with barcode scan, auto-GRN and supplier portal update
N/A

functional.text: How is the identity of incoming raw material verified against the purchase order?

options.text: No verification; material accepted as delivered
Visual inspection by store keeper against paper PO
Checklist-based verification with manual sign-off
System-based PO matching with quantity and grade check
Automated PO-to-GRN reconciliation with supplier certificate upload
N/A

functional.text: How is quantity measurement and weighing of incoming material handled?

options.text: No weighing; quantity accepted per supplier's bill
Manual weighing with physical scales; entered in register
Digital weighbridge with print-out; data entered manually in ERP
Weighbridge integrated with ERP for automatic GRN quantity
IoT-enabled weighbridge with real-time data feed, auto-alerts for short receipt
N/A

functional.text: How is incoming material quality checked at the time of receipt?

options.text: No QC at receipt; quality checked only after processing
Visual check by store keeper; rejected if obviously defective
Formal sampling/testing done with paper-based QC report
Digital QC report linked to GRN; quarantine flag raised for rejects
Statistical sampling with digital CAPA trigger; supplier scorecard updated automatically
N/A

functional.text: How are supplier test certificates / COA (Certificate of Analysis) managed?

options.text: Not collected or not reviewed
Collected in physical file but not linked to material lot
Scanned and saved in shared folder manually
Uploaded in ERP/QMS against each GRN
Automated COA extraction with parameter validation against specs
N/A

functional.text: How is non-conforming or rejected incoming material handled?

options.text: Sent back verbally or mixed with accepted stock
Physically segregated but no formal record
Manual rejection note raised and filed
Digital NCMR raised in ERP; return or rework decision tracked
Automated rejection workflow with supplier debit note and corrective action loop
N/A

functional.text: How is material labelling and identification done at inwarding?

options.text: No labelling; identified by location
Manual handwritten tags
Printed labels with basic info (name, date, quantity)
Barcode labels generated from ERP at GRN
QR/RFID labels with full traceability to supplier batch and test results
N/A

functional.text: How is the unloading and material movement from gate to store tracked?

options.text: No tracking; unloading team handles informally
Manual tallying by store keeper
Material movement slip raised manually
ERP material transfer document created on movement
Digital MHE (material handling equipment) log with GPS or RFID tracking
N/A

functional.text: How is supplier performance on delivery compliance tracked?

options.text: Not tracked
Informal notes on late deliveries
Manual delivery performance log updated monthly
ERP-based on-time delivery % and short-delivery reporting
Automated supplier scorecard with delivery, quality and lead-time KPIs
N/A

functional.text: How are advance shipping notifications (ASN) or dispatch alerts managed?

options.text: No advance notice; surprises at gate
Phone or WhatsApp intimation only
Email with challan details before arrival
Supplier portal with ASN creation before dispatch
EDI/API-based real-time dispatch notification with material details
N/A

functional.text: How are material shortages at inwarding escalated to procurement?

options.text: Not escalated; handled by store keeper alone
Phone call to purchase team
Email raised with shortage details
ERP-generated alert to procurement on GRN shortfall
Automated replenishment trigger sent to approved supplier based on shortage logic
N/A

functional.text: How is hazardous or restricted material identified and segregated at inwarding?

options.text: No special process; treated as regular material
Store keeper uses experience/judgment
Material safety data sheet (MSDS) checked manually
MSDS linked to material master in ERP; special handling instructions displayed
Digital safety workflow with mandatory MSDS review, PPE checklist, and CPCB/regulation compliance check
N/A

functional.text: How is inwarding data used for production planning or demand forecasting?

options.text: No connection between inwarding and production planning
Production team manually checks what has arrived
Daily GRN summary shared via Excel with planning team
ERP auto-updates inventory; planning module considers inward stock
Real-time inventory feed from GRN drives MRP and production scheduling automatically
N/A

functional.text: How is inwarding cycle time (gate to bin) measured and improved?

options.text: Not measured
Supervisor estimates informally
Manual time log at key steps
ERP timestamps at GRN, QC release and bin storage
Digital process time analysis with bottleneck alerts and continuous improvement dashboard
N/A

functional.text: How is temperature or condition-sensitive material monitored during inwarding and initial storage?

options.text: No special monitoring
Visual inspection only at receipt
Manual temperature recording at receipt
Digital data-logger checked at inward; result logged in QMS
IoT temperature sensor on vehicle/container with automated acceptance/rejection trigger
N/A

functional.text: How is warehouse storage location (binning) currently managed?

options.text: No defined locations; material kept anywhere available
Locations marked physically on floor/racks but no system
Excel-based bin register maintained manually
ERP/WMS with fixed bin locations per material
Dynamic slotting WMS with weight, velocity and dimension-based bin assignment
N/A

functional.text: How is FIFO (First-In-First-Out) compliance ensured in the warehouse?

options.text: Not followed; material issued randomly
Store keeper maintains FIFO by memory/experience
Date labels on materials; staff trained on FIFO
WMS enforces FIFO; system suggests oldest batch first
Automated FIFO control via RFID/barcode with operator alerts for out-of-sequence picks
N/A

functional.text: How is inventory quantity accuracy maintained in the warehouse?

options.text: Physical counting done annually only
Monthly physical count reconciled with register
Cycle counting process defined; deviations noted
ERP-based perpetual inventory with discrepancy reporting
Real-time inventory accuracy with RFID/barcode; automated cycle count triggers
N/A

functional.text: How is material issued from the warehouse to production?

options.text: Informal verbal request; no documentation
Manual material requisition slip filled by production
Store keeper issues against signed requisition; Excel updated
ERP material issue against production order; stock auto-deducted
Pull-based digital kanban or automated material call from shop floor
N/A

Annual review by purchase manager
Quarterly Excel analysis of non-moving items
ERP generates slow-moving report; management reviews monthly
AI-powered inventory health dashboard with automatic disposal/redeployment recommendations
N/A

functional.text: How is the controlled storage environment (temperature, humidity) for sensitive materials managed?

options.text: No controlled storage; all material kept in open warehouse
Separate room used but no monitoring instruments
Manual temperature/humidity log maintained
Calibrated digital monitoring with records in QMS
IoT sensors with continuous monitoring, automated alarms and HVAC integration
N/A

options.text: No shelf life tracking
Store keeper checks labels periodically
Excel tracker with expiry dates; reviewed monthly
ERP auto-alert when material nears shelf life threshold
System blocks issuance of expired material; auto-triggers disposal/return workflow
N/A

options.text: No measurement; space issues handled informally
Visual estimate by warehouse manager
Manual occupancy map updated periodically
WMS with space utilisation reporting by location/zone
3D warehouse simulation with AI-driven slotting and space optimisation
N/A

options.text: Returned material placed anywhere; no formal process
Separate area used but no documentation
Manual return note raised and entered in register
ERP return to store document raised; stock reinstated
Barcode-scanned return process with automated condition check and restocking routing

Manual logbook for usage and maintenance
Excel-based maintenance schedule
Digital preventive maintenance system with alerts
IoT-enabled MHE with real-time utilisation data, predictive maintenance and operator assignment

Separate area maintained manually
Independent Excel register for packing materials
Separate material type in ERP with reorder level
Automated consumption tracking linked to production orders; auto-replenishment triggered

Physical lock with key; no log maintained
Gated entry with manual visitor/staff register
Biometric or card-based access log maintained
CCTV with AI motion detection, digital access log and exception alerts

options.text: Informal movement without documentation
Physical transfer slip used
Manual transfer voucher entered in Excel
ERP transfer order raised between storage locations
Barcode-triggered transfer with real-time stock update and route tracking

options.text: Layout never reviewed; unchanged for years
Reviewed informally when problems arise
Annual manual review with floor plan update
WMS-based velocity analysis drives annual slotting review
Continuous AI-driven layout optimisation based on pick frequency, weight and safety rules

functional.text: How is warehouse KPI (on-time issue, accuracy, cycle count variance) reporting done?

options.text: No KPIs tracked
Verbal updates from store keeper
Monthly manual report prepared in Excel
ERP-generated warehouse KPI dashboard reviewed by management
Real-time live dashboard with drill-down analytics, trend charts and AI recommendations

functional.text: How is the production plan created and communicated to the shop floor?

options.text: Verbal Instruction from owner/manager daily
Handwritten daily production schedule on whiteboard
Excel-based weekly plan shared via WhatsApp/email
ERP production order generated and dispatched digitally
AI-driven production schedule linked to demand forecast, material availability and machine capacity

options.text: Not captured; machines repaired when they stop
Operator verbally informs supervisor; no record
Manual breakdown register maintained
Digital breakdown log with downtime reason codes in ERP/MES
IoT machine sensors with automatic downtime detection, root cause classification and MTTR tracking

functional.text: How is shift-wise production output recorded and reported?

options.text: No shift reporting; only end-of-day count
Physical tally sheet filled by operator
Excel sheet submitted by supervisor per shift
Digital shift report in MES/ERP with actual vs. planned comparison
Real-time counter or SCADA-linked automated production count with live dashboard

options.text: Owner/supervisor decides verbally
Physical shift roster on notice board
Excel-based shift schedule updated weekly
Digital shift management in HRMS/ERP with operator skills matrix
AI-optimised shift allocation based on skill, absenteeism data and machine requirements

functional.text: How is raw material delivery to the shop floor (from store to machine) managed?

options.text: Operator walks to store whenever needed
Supervisor calls store keeper when material runs low
Pre-planned material kit issued at start of shift
ERP-generated material requisition linked to production order
Automated kanban or AGV-driven material replenishment to machine side

options.text: No formal planning; whoever is present is deployed
Experience-based judgment by production manager
Excel-based headcount plan reviewed weekly
ERP workforce planning module aligned with production orders
AI-driven manpower demand model integrating absenteeism patterns, OEE and production plan

options.text: No in-process monitoring; quality checked at end
Operator self-inspection with visual check
QC inspector checks at defined frequency; paper record
Digital in-process quality log in QMS linked to production order
Automated SPC (Statistical Process Control) with real-time control charts and auto-alarm
